intro.program_admin_contact: Aditi Misra, Ph.D., CU Denver (email: aditi.misra@ucdenver.edu)

en.deployment_name: Federal Boulevard BRT Corridor Travel Survey
en.summary_line_1: This is a Colorado DOT sponsored project with a goal to make the upcoming BRT service accessible and useful for everyone
en.short_textual_description: The proposed project will focus on communities along the BRT corridor of Federal Blvd. The purpose of the proposed project is twofold: (i) to understand the current barriers to transit use, such as a lack of bus stop infrastructure, safety concerns, first and last-mile connectivity issues, etc.; and (ii) to identify solutions that best address the needs of the community. The aim is to complement and expand the scope of CDOT’s ongoing work towards making the BRT project successful and equitable. The proposed BRT line runs through very diverse communities, both in terms of race and income, so understanding the unique needs and concerns of the communities impacted is essential. A study engaging community members and their direct input can help ensure that the effects of BRT introduction are equitably distributed in the short and long term.
en.why_we_collect: This project aims to understand the current travel pattern of the residents within a 0.5 mi radius of Federal Boulevard
